rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/record/allure.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) JS/TS allure-call discovery: JS pattern `\ballure\.` -> `\bxallure\.` KILLED (exact set(tags) equality + tags['DI-2'] == the .ts file); (2) Java @Story/@Feature discovery: `@(Story|Feature)` -> `@(XStory|XFeature)` KILLED (tags['DI-3'] == the .java file); (3) conventional test-file names, probed in BOTH directions: dropping `*.test.ts` from TEST_FILE_GLOBS KILLED (the TS test vanishes) and adding `*.txt` KILLED (notes.txt's allure.story('DI-9') prose would be scanned -- the non-test-file-NOT-scanned negative is pinned by the exact set equality); (4) function-scope capture for Python: `ast.get_source_segment(text, node) or ""` -> `text` KILLED ('def helper' must be OUTSIDE the pinned source while 'def test_py' is inside); (5) whole-file scope for other languages: non-Python branch `append(text)` -> `append("")` KILLED (sources['DI-2']/['DI-3'] asserted equal to the full file text byte-for-byte). Purpose clause (tag-linkage warnings, audit coverage, faithfulness hashing in polyglot repos) is exercised via the real shared path: the tested scan_source_tags/scan_tagged_sources are exactly what story_audit/audit.py:234, design_gate.py:327 and record/faithfulness.py:143,154 consume; downstream reporting is covered by those features' own tagged tests.
